last_item: Mode-S convergence check (backlog actionable-empty: only a NEEDS-BACKEND backup-badge item + two low/watch items remained). Ran a 5-agent parallel discovery panel covering every view + nav/IA + first-run + cross-view consistency, each given the full already-shipped/already-rejected digest and a deliberately HIGH bar (genuinely new, high-or-med-payoff, non-over-design only).
last_result: CONVERGED — all 5 agents independently returned 'NO new high/med item'. Coverage: (1) Dashboard+Query, (2) Roles&DBs+Backups+BackupStorageForm, (3) Alerts+Migrate, (4) Settings+DatabaseTuning+Pooler+Login, (5) nav/IA+first-run+cross-view consistency. They cited the mature state: graduated destructive gates (not warning-on-warning), honest state surfacing (StaleBanner, 'limited for safety'), consistent shadcn primitives + terminology across views, empty states paired with actionable hints, backup config co-located (iter 11). Agent 3 probed six candidate nits (cluster 'Exclude' field lacks a FieldDescription the single-db target has; 'Send test' disabled-state relies on a hover title; etc.) and judged each restraint-negative or zero-payoff. No code shipped, docs-only commit, no hard gates needed. Per the contract (backlog actionable-empty AND fresh discovery pass surfaced no high/med item) this is the FIRST stable_streak increment -> 1/3. Two more clean convergence passes -> write COMPLETE.md and stop the loop. Next iteration: run another Mode-S convergence check; do NOT manufacture low-value work from the low/watch items to avoid converging (converging early is a win).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
